runs.content:
Week of 2026-07-23

Business Breakdowns
Host: Matt Reustle & Zack Fuss (Colossus)
Why for Fred: The Colossus network sibling to Invest Like the Best and Acquired -- but where Acquired runs 5-8 hour marathons, Business Breakdowns dissects one company per episode in 45-60 minutes: origin, business model, unit economics, competitive moat. The tighter, more frequent format for the same capital-allocation and moat-analysis lens Fred already applies via Berkshire/Buffett principles, without Acquired's multi-week time commitment.
Format: Long-form interview (single-business deep dive), weekly, ~45-60 min
Sample episode: Any recent European or industrial-company breakdown -- check the feed for a name overlapping Fred's portfolio or watchlist sectors
Link: https://www.joincolossus.com/business-breakdowns

Me, Myself, and AI
Host: Sam Ransbotham (MIT Sloan Management Review)
Why for Fred: Fred's AI list so far skews research/capital-allocation (Dwarkesh, No Priors) or engineering (Latent Space, MLST) -- none cover enterprise AI adoption from the inside. Ransbotham interviews executives actually running AI programs at large employers (recent guests include Bank of America on workforce upskilling, Wendy's, Cisco, and OpenAI's in-house economist on labour-market effects). Direct match for the "AI impact on work" thread in Fred's profile, from the operator side.
Format: Long-form interview, biweekly, ~30-40 min
Sample episode: Season 13 finale (June 16, 2026) -- Bank of America on reskilling a global workforce for AI
Link: https://sloanreview.mit.edu/audio-series/me-myself-and-ai/

On The Margin
Host: Michael Ippolito & Tyler Neville (Blockworks)
Why for Fred: Bankless, Epicenter, and Empire (all previously suggested, none picked up) approach DeFi from inside the crypto-native world. On The Margin sits at the seam between macro finance and crypto -- Ippolito interviews traditional asset managers and macro strategists about institutional money treating digital assets as a macro instrument, then closes the week with a co-hosted macro+crypto roundup. Treats the two domains as one conversation instead of two separate hobbies.
Format: Two-part show -- guest interview + roundup, twice weekly, ~45-60 min
Sample episode: "Bitcoin's Role in the Debt End Game" -- Jonah Van Bourg on sovereign debt dynamics and crypto as a macro hedge
Link: https://blockworks.co/podcast/onthemargin

Writing Excuses
Host: Mary Robinette Kowal, Dan Wells, Erin Roberts, DongWon Song, Howard Tayler
Why for Fred: Scriptnotes (suggested last week) covers film/TV screenwriting craft; Writing Excuses is the working-fiction-writer equivalent -- a rotating panel of published novelists and audio-drama veterans (Kowal is also a professional audiobook narrator) dissecting one narrow craft problem per episode. Built to be "fifteen minutes long, because you're in a hurry" -- immediately actionable, mapping more directly onto ongoing production work (Lightfin, The Optimised Jungle) than longer discussion-format shows already on the list.
Format: Panel discussion, weekly, ~15-20 min
Sample episode: Any episode tagged "structure" or "worldbuilding" -- check the archive for one matching a current Lightfin story problem
Link: https://writingexcuses.com/

Week of 2026-07-16

This Week in Intelligent Investing
Host: John Mihaljevic (MOI Global) & Elliot Turner
Why for Fred: The only show on this list that is literally the MOI Global podcast rather than a show that shares its guest pool -- Mihaljevic chairs MOI Global itself, and the show pulls directly from the same practitioner network (Chris Bloomstran, Phil Ordway, and recent guest Simon Kold, a Copenhagen-based European value investor) that underlies his stated MOI Global interest. Distinct from the institutional tier (Capital Allocators, previously suggested) or academic tier (Value Investing with Legends, previously suggested): this is the actual working community discussing live theses.
Format: Long-form interview, weekly, ~60-75 min
Sample episode: Simon Kold episode -- European value investor discussing business-quality evaluation
Link: https://twiii.podbean.com/

Latent Space: The AI Engineer Podcast
Host: Alessio Fanelli (Decibel Partners) & swyx (Shawn Wang)
Why for Fred: Where Dwarkesh, MLST, and No Priors (all previously suggested) cover AI from a research or capital-allocation angle, Latent Space is the AI engineering show -- it goes deepest on inference economics, agent infrastructure, and production deployment, matching Fred's stated interest in inference trends and AI startups rather than AI as abstract capability discussion.
Format: Long-form interview, weekly, ~60-90 min
Sample episode: Any 2026 episode on inference economics or AI agent infrastructure
Link: https://www.latent.space/podcast

Behavioral Grooves
Host: Kurt Nelson, PhD & Tim Houlihan
Why for Fred: A longer-form (60+ min) companion to Choiceology (previously suggested, ~30 min narrative format) -- goes deeper and slower with working behavioral economists, in the same register as Seth Godin and Kevin Kelly's thinking on human behaviour. A January 2026 episode features Alex Imas, the same economist Fred already heard on Dwarkesh's "What remains scarce after AGI?" episode, discussing loss aversion and the winner's curse.
Format: Long-form interview, weekly, ~60 min
Sample episode: Alex Imas episode (Jan 26, 2026) -- "The Winner's Curse"
Link: https://behavioralgrooves.com/

Scriptnotes
Host: John August & Craig Mazin
Why for Fred: The first suggestion to land in the Creative/writing-storytelling domain, which six months of prior recommendations skipped entirely. 500+ episodes deep, Mazin (Emmy winner for Chernobyl, showrunner of The Last of Us) and August dissect narrative structure, character, and dialogue with working screenwriters and showrunners -- craft discussion relevant to both the Lightfin children's audio project and the structural comedy work behind The Optimised Jungle.
Format: Interview + duo discussion, weekly, ~45-75 min
Sample episode: Ryan Condal (showrunner, House of the Dragon) episode -- writers'-room mechanics for long-running serialised story
Link: https://johnaugust.com/podcast
